commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Solicitous. Or ambitious. Hebrew, "the potent "or Septuagint, "the impious."

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh When a wicked man dies, his expectation shall perish: and the hope of unjust men perishes. “When a righteous man dies, hope does not perish. ” He hopes that his children will do well; he hopes to be provided with great things. This passage also transports us to thoughts of the resurrection or of our posterity. Or, since one who is righteous has delighted in all these things already, he will also enjoy their future consummation; or, finally, that he would have enjoyment of glory after death. Commentary on the Proverbs of Solomon, Fragment.
